title: Kulichy
description: Kulichy are traditional Ukrainian fermented pastries that are crispy on the outside and soft on the inside. They are often served as a snack or appetizer and are said to have hangover-curing properties due to their high probiotic content.
instructions: Combine warm milk, yeast, and 1 teaspoon sugar in a bowl. Let the mixture sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
Add flour, salt, and remaining sugar to the bowl and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Preheat a deep frying pan with about 2-3 inches of high heat cooking oil to 375F.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Heat a non-stick skillet or frying pan over medium heat and cook the kulichy for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Drain the kulichy on paper towels and serve warm.
Serve with a dollop of sour cream or a sprinkle of chives, if desired.
